symptoms.symptom_name: Movement-triggered nausea/vertigo with cold sweats and pallor after a kinetic trigger

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear kinetic trigger with head-movement-provoked nausea and autonomic features is characteristic of kinetosis.
A kinetic trigger favours kinetosis but does not by itself exclude a coincidental central event.
Absence of headache, focal deficits, dysarthria and diplopia lowers stroke probability but cannot exclude it without examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal neuro exam: no nystagmus at rest, no dysmetria, intact cranial nerves (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal focused neuro exam argues against a central cause but is a clinician examination not available via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unable to keep fluids down / reduced urine output

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dehydration is the complication driving escalation, not a diagnostic discriminator.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Clinical diagnosis when kinetic trigger clear and central screen negative.
Only if central features develop / fails to settle — via ED, not telehealth.
